outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the social determinants of health, health inequalities and the role of nursing in public health
Critically evaluate evidence underpinning public health approaches and their application to nursing practice
Communicate effectively with peers in a collaborative group presentation setting
Demonstrate awareness of professional responsibilities, ethical considerations and societal influences in public health nursing
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of human anatomy, physiology and body systems relevant to nursing practice
Apply understanding of homeostasis, pathophysiology and biosciences to the assessment of nursing care needs
Use anatomical and physiological knowledge to underpin clinical decision-making
Demonstrate commitment to building and maintaining scientific knowledge as a foundation for evidence-based nursing
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of evidence-based practice and the research underpinning safe and effective nursing
Critically appraise research evidence and apply findings to inform nursing care and clinical decision-making
Apply evidence-based knowledge to the care of people with commonly encountered physical health conditions
Commit to lifelong learning and keeping knowledge and skills up to date through engagement with evidence

title: Collaborative Care Planning
note: Unit specification PDF was empty; assessment details sourced from Canvas assignment brief

assessments.type: Care plan (APIE format, individual, based on provided case study)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge of collaborative care planning principles and person-centred approaches
Apply collaborative and interprofessional working principles to nursing care planning
Demonstrate person-centred communication and collaborative care planning skills
Work effectively with others and demonstrate professional accountability in the care planning process

assessments.type: Mandatory training attendance (BLS, IPC, Moving and Handling)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate foundational knowledge and understanding of communication and relationship management in nursing contexts
Apply clinical skills and nursing procedures in simulated practice settings
Demonstrate professional values and accountability appropriate to a student nurse on simulated placement
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the professional, ethical and values-based foundations of nursing practice, including The Code
Critically analyse the values, ethics and regulatory frameworks that underpin professional nursing identity
Demonstrate person-centred, values-based care in interactions with patients, families and colleagues
Demonstrate personal integrity, ethical conduct and cultural competence as an emerging nursing professional

assessments.type: Portfolio (3 reflections, SWOT, PDP, other-field exposure)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate understanding of the emerging role of the nurse and professional growth, including reflective practice
Demonstrate the ability to reflect analytically on personal development and produce a professional development plan
Demonstrate essential drug dosage calculation competence using the SafeMedicate platform
Demonstrate professional maturity, self-directed learning and effective communication
Demonstrate and apply knowledge of biosciences, pathophysiology and pharmacology to support clinical decision-making
Apply bioscientific knowledge to critically analyse nursing assessment and care planning
Respond proactively to signs of deterioration and apply bioscience knowledge to clinical nursing practice
Demonstrate commitment to applying scientific knowledge to safe, evidence-based nursing practice

title: Mental Health and Mental Ill Health in Children and Young People

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of mental health, mental ill health and the factors affecting the mental wellbeing of children and young people
Critically analyse evidence relating to mental health assessment, care planning and therapeutic approaches for CYP
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to support CYP with mental health challenges in practice
Demonstrate cultural competence, advocacy and collaborative working in the context of CYP mental health

title: Developing Knowledge and Skills for Nursing Practice (Semester 1)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate and apply knowledge of commonly encountered health conditions, medicines and treatments when undertaking nursing assessments and developing person-centred care plans
Critically analyse evidence to inform nursing interventions for people with complex care needs
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to provide evidence-based nursing care including nutrition, hydration, skin integrity, symptom management and discharge planning
Work effectively in teams, demonstrate professional values and contribute to the interdisciplinary care of people

title: Developing Knowledge and Skills for Nursing Practice (Semester 2)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate and apply knowledge of commonly encountered health conditions, medicines and treatments when undertaking nursing assessments and developing person-centred care plans
Critically analyse evidence to inform nursing interventions for people with complex care needs
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to provide evidence-based nursing care including nutrition, hydration, skin integrity, symptom management and discharge planning
Work effectively in teams, demonstrate professional values and contribute to the interdisciplinary care of people
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of dementia, its impact on individuals, families and carers, and the legal, ethical and governance frameworks underpinning dementia care
Critically evaluate creative and empowering approaches to dementia care within the evidence base
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to provide person-centred, creative and compassionate care for people living with dementia
Demonstrate empathy, cultural competence and advocacy in dementia care

title: Principles of Adult Critical and Intensive Care Nursing

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the principles, pathophysiology and clinical challenges encountered in critical and intensive care settings
Critically evaluate the evidence base underpinning nursing assessment and care in critical care environments
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills required for nursing care in a critical care context
Reflect on professional practice in critical care and demonstrate commitment to high standards of patient safety

title: Palliative and End of Life Care

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of palliative care philosophy, end of life care needs and the ethical and legal frameworks underpinning this area of practice
Critically analyse the evidence base for palliative and end of life nursing interventions
Communicate effectively with dying patients, their families and carers in end of life care situations
Demonstrate advocacy, compassion and cultural sensitivity in palliative care practice

title: Introduction to Neonatal Low Dependency Care

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of neonatal anatomy, physiology, development and the care needs of neonates requiring low dependency care
Apply bioscientific and clinical knowledge to the assessment and planning of care for neonates
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills required to care for neonates in a low dependency setting
Demonstrate family-centred care principles and advocacy for neonates and their families

title: Voices in Care: Doing Experience Driven Research

Critically evaluate qualitative research methods, rigour, analytical techniques and underpinning evidence
Demonstrate ability to undertake and apply appropriate standards to a small, low-risk qualitative research project
Reflect on own knowledge and skills in qualitative research; demonstrate understanding of research limitations; participate in collaborative learning

Demonstrate initiative and autonomy in portfolio development; demonstrate medication measurement skills

title: Advancing Care: Social and Political Context

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the social, political and policy contexts that shape nursing practice and health care delivery
Critically analyse the impact of health policy, social inequalities and political processes on nursing practice and patient outcomes
Apply understanding of health policy and social context to advocate for patients and communities in nursing practice
Demonstrate political awareness and the ability to engage with policy processes as an emerging nursing professional

title: Nurse as Inquirer of Clinical Evidence (NICE)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of research methodology, evidence hierarchies and the application of research to nursing practice
Critically appraise and synthesise a body of research evidence relevant to a nursing inquiry question
Demonstrate advanced research literacy through the production of a critical annotated bibliography
Demonstrate commitment to using evidence to improve nursing practice and patient outcomes

title: Nurse as Leader, Manager and Coordinator of Care

assessments.type: Written assignment (reflective with personal development plan)

outcomes.text: Understand principles of leadership, management and team dynamics; demonstrate person and team management; apply evidence-based approaches for communication and interdisciplinary working; understand health economics and policy impact on care

Understand roles in the interdisciplinary team; apply performance management; monitor quality; understand mechanisms for influencing policy; contribute to supervision and team reflection; exhibit leadership potential

Use digital technologies; facilitate safe discharge and care transition; lead and manage nursing care; facilitate equitable access; identify and manage risks; identify and reflect on critical incidents
Demonstrate enhanced IT skills, communication with diverse people, team working and facilitation skills to promote patient safety

assessments.type: Portfolio (3 reflections, SWOT, PDP, other-field exposure)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate understanding of the professional role of a registered nurse, professional development and reflective practice at the point of registration
Demonstrate analytical reflection on personal and professional development as an emerging registered nurse
Demonstrate fitness to practise and professional accountability through portfolio completion
Demonstrate readiness for registration and commitment to lifelong professional development

assessments.type: Situated Learning in Practice (coaching session + learner support + critical reflection)
Innovation for Practice Education (innovation 'Spark' presentation with 500-word rationale)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of how practice learning is organised; teaching, learning and assessment theories; roles of practice supervisors and assessors; factors influencing student integration; learning culture and public safety; and systems for educational governance and quality

Critically appreciate political and professional issues in nurse education; demonstrate critical evaluation, synthesis and originality in applying evidence; draw reasoned conclusions and engage with advanced scholarship; act with professional integrity in achievement of NMC (2018) standards

Demonstrate interpersonal communication skills for student learning and assessment; prioritise and adapt to personalised learning needs; provide objective, evidence-based supervision and constructive feedback; design formal and informal educational experiences; critically reflect on own development as practitioner, supervisor and educator

Act as a professional role model; exercise initiative and academic curiosity for CPD and scholarly output; manage independent study and professional practice demands; use information technology effectively; demonstrate accomplished communication; work co-operatively as a member of a team
